zh.lifeSummary: Jane Stones是Henry Stones的女兒，於1503年出生在英國藍卡爾法爾沃斯。她於大約1520年與Alexander Hulton在英國藍卡爾法爾沃斯結婚。他們至少是5個兒子和4個女兒的父母。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: Jane Stones是Henry Stones的女儿，于1503年出生在英国藍卡爾法爾沃斯。她于大约1520年与Alexander Hulton在英国藍卡爾法爾沃斯结婚。他们至少是5个儿子和4个女儿的父母。

nameMeanings.name: Stones
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (Yorkshire and Lancashire): variant of Stone with plural or post-medieval excrescent -s. A number of the places called Stone are sometimes recorded with plural spellings in the medieval period, including Stone (Staffordshire), recorded as Stanes in 1187.

variants.name: Stone
meanings: English: from Middle English ston(e) ‘stone, rock’ (Old English stān). The surname may be topographic, for someone who lived on stony ground, by a notable outcrop of rock, or by a stone boundary-marker or monument, or habitational, from a place called Stone, such as those in Buckinghamshire, Devon, Gloucestershire, Hampshire, Kent, Somerset, Staffordshire, and Worcestershire.
Irish (Kilkenny): adopted for Irish Ó Clochartaigh (see Clougherty ) and/or Ó Clochasaigh (see Clohessy ), and possibly several other names containing or thought to contain the element cloch ‘stone’.
Americanized form (translation into English) of various surnames in other languages, meaning ‘stone’, including Jewish Stein , Norwegian Steine, French Lapierre .

nameMeanings.name: Jane
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Originally a feminine form of John , from the Old French form Je(h)anne. Since the 17th century it has proved the most popular of the feminine forms of John, ahead of Joan and Jean . It now also commonly occurs as the second element in combinations such as Sarah-Jane. In Britain it is still one of the most frequent of all girls' names. It is not a royal name, but was borne by the tragic Lady Jane Grey ( 1537–54 ), who was unwillingly proclaimed queen in 1553 , deposed nine days later, and executed the following year. Seventy years earlier, the name had come into prominence as that of Jane Shore , mistress of King Edward IV and subsequently of Thomas Grey , 1st Marquess of Dorset, Lady Jane's grandfather. Jane Shore's tribulations in 1483 at the hands of Richard III , Edward's brother and successor, became the subject of popular ballads and plays, which may well have increased the currency of the name in the 16th century. A 19th-century influence was its use as the name of the central character in Charlotte Brontë's novel Jane Eyre ( 1847 ). From 1932 to 1959 it was used as the name of a cheerful and scantily clad beauty whose adventures were chronicled in a strip cartoon in the Daily Mirror. It is also borne by the American film stars Jane Russell ( 1921–2011 ) and Jane Fonda ( b. 1937 ).

variants.name: Jayne
meanings: Elaborated spelling of   Jane  , a form popularized no doubt by the American actress  Jayne Mansfield  (  Vera Jayne Palmer ,  1933–67 ) and British ice-dancer  Jayne Torvill  (  b. 1957 ).

variants.name: Jain
meanings: Originally a feminine form of John , from the Old French form Je(h)anne. Since the 17th century it has proved the most popular of the feminine forms of John, ahead of Joan and Jean . It now also commonly occurs as the second element in combinations such as Sarah-Jane. In Britain it is still one of the most frequent of all girls' names. It is not a royal name, but was borne by the tragic Lady Jane Grey ( 1537–54 ), who was unwillingly proclaimed queen in 1553 , deposed nine days later, and executed the following year. Seventy years earlier, the name had come into prominence as that of Jane Shore , mistress of King Edward IV and subsequently of Thomas Grey , 1st Marquess of Dorset, Lady Jane's grandfather. Jane Shore's tribulations in 1483 at the hands of Richard III , Edward's brother and successor, became the subject of popular ballads and plays, which may well have increased the currency of the name in the 16th century. A 19th-century influence was its use as the name of the central character in Charlotte Brontë's novel Jane Eyre ( 1847 ). From 1932 to 1959 it was used as the name of a cheerful and scantily clad beauty whose adventures were chronicled in a strip cartoon in the Daily Mirror. It is also borne by the American film stars Jane Russell ( 1921–2011 ) and Jane Fonda ( b. 1937 ).

historicEvents.title: Book of Common Prayer
historicEvents.description: Book of Common Prayer, a product of the English Reformation, was published in 1549 for assistance in the administration of the sacraments and other rites and ceremonies of the church according to the use of the Church of England. The book outlined morning, evening, and communion prayers and orders for baptism and marriage, thus making England a truly Protestant state.

historicEvents.title: Act of Uniformity
historicEvents.description: The Act of Uniformity was passed by the Parliament of England and required all people to go to church once a week. The consequence of not attending church was a fine of 12 pence, which was a considerable amount for a poor person.

historicEvents.title: State Lottery
historicEvents.description: A State Lottery was recorded in 1569. The tickets were sold at St. Paul's Cathedral in London.

historicEvents.title: The Royal Exchange is Organized
historicEvents.description: The Royal Exchange was organized in January 1571 by Sir Thomas Gresham. Gresham is known as the father of English banking.

historicEvents.title: The Honourable East India Company
historicEvents.description: The Honourable East India Company, a British joint-stock company, was established in 1600 for trade in the Indian Ocean region. At its height, the British East India Company had a private army which was twice the size of the British Army, ruled large sections of India, and revenues in the millions.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Historic UK - The East India Company

historicEvents.title: The Pilgrims Board the Mayflower
historicEvents.description: "The Pilgrims boarded the Mayflower in search of religious freedom in the New World in September 1620. The Mayflower set sail from Plymouth, England, with 102 passengers, 40 of whom called themselves ""Saints."" After two stormy months at sea, the ship reached the New World. Nearly half of the group died during their first winter due to malnutrition and the harsh New England winter."

en.lifeSummary: Jane Stones was born in 1503, in Farnworth, Lancashire, England as the daughter of Henry Stones. She married Alexander Hulton about 1520, in Farnworth, Lancashire, England. They were the parents of at least 5 sons and 4 daughters.

fr.lifeSummary: Jane Stones est née en 1503, à Farnworth, Lancashire, Angleterre, fille de Henry Stones. Elle a épousé Alexander Hulton environ 1520, à Farnworth, Lancashire, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 5 fils et 4 filles.

de.lifeSummary: Jane Stones wurde 1503 in Farnworth, Lancashire, England, als Tochter von Henry Stones geboren. Sie hat um 1520 in Farnworth, Lancashire, England Alexander Hulton geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 5 Söhne und 4 Töchter.

it.lifeSummary: Jane Stones è nata il nel 1503 nel Farnworth, Lancashire, England come figlia di Henry Stones. Lei sposato Alexander Hulton circa 1520, nel Farnworth, Lancashire, England. Erano genitori di almeno 5 figli e 4 figlie.

ja.lifeSummary: Jane Stonesは1503年にイギリスランカシュアイアファルンウオルスでHenry Stonesの娘として生まれた。約1520年にイギリスランカシュアイアファルンウオルスでAlexander Hultonと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも5人の息子と4人の娘の親であった。

ko.lifeSummary: Jane Stones(은)는 1503년 영국 랭카실르 패른워스에서 Henry Stones의 딸로 태어났다. 그녀는 대략 1520년 영국 랭카실르 패른워스에서 Alexander Hulton(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 다섯 명의 아들과 네 명의 딸을 두었다.

pt.lifeSummary: Jane Stones nasceu em 1503, em Farnworth, Lancashire, Inglaterra filha de Henry Stones. Ela casou-se com Alexander Hulton aproximadamente 1520, em Farnworth, Lancashire, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 5 filhos e 4 filhas.

ru.lifeSummary: Jane Stones родилась в 1503 г. в местности фарнворт, ланкашир, Англия. Её отца звали Henry Stones. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Alexander Hulton около 1520 г. в местности фарнворт, ланкашир, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 5 сыновей и 4 дочери.

es.lifeSummary: Jane Stones nació en 1503, en Farnworth, Lancashire, Inglaterra como hija de Henry Stones. Se casó con Alexander Hulton aproximadamente 1520, en Farnworth, Lancashire, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 5 hijos y 4 hijas.
